perturbation.original_text: 1.19 "FTE" shall mean a fully allocated employee or consultant of BII and working on the Technology transfer with such time and effort to constitute the equivalent of one (1) employee on a full time basis consistent with normal business and scientific practice […***…].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.19 "FTE" shall mean a person employed by BII.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the detailed definition of FTE and its involvement in technology transfer, while the agreement continues to reference FTEs in the context of technology transfer support (Section 5.2.3), this creates an in-text contradiction because the specific parameters for what constitutes an FTE's involvement are undefined, making it difficult to ascertain the level of support BII is obligated to provide and how to calculate the payment according to Section 5.2.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Technology Access Fee includes Technology transfer support of [...***...] FTEs of BII for a period of [...***...] for each of the [...***...] FTEs in a time frame of [...***...] beginning with XENCOR's written request to use or have used (e.g. by a Business Partner) the Process outside the terms and conditions set forth in a contract manufacturing agreement with BII.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition of 'FTE' as it relates to 'technology transfer' and the equivalent of one (1) employee on a full time basis. The contradicted text refers to providing support of a specific number of FTEs. Without the original definition, there is no objective way to measure the level of support that should be provided, and so a key term, FTE, is used without a definition. Therefore, there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.42 "Specification(s)" shall mean all the tests, analytical methods and/or limits, and the results thereof, as applicable, agreed by the Parties, within which the Product has to conform to be considered acceptable by XENCOR for clinical use set forth in Appendix 6. The Parties are in agreement, that in the first instance they will agree on preliminary specifications which shall then be fixed to final Specifications in accordance with Section 2.5.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.42 "Specification(s)" shall mean all the tests, analytical methods and/or limits, and the results thereof, as applicable, agreed by the Parties, within which the Product has to conform to be considered acceptable by XENCOR for clinical use.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Appendix 6, which should contain the actual specifications, and the link to Section 2.5 for agreeing on preliminary specifications, a contradiction is introduced. Section 4.1 still refers to 'Acceptance Criteria' and whether the Product meets the specifications, but now there is no clear contractual basis for those specifications if Appendix 6 doesn't exist and the agreement on preliminary specifications has been omitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Delivery of Product by BII shall be made [...***...] BII Facility (Incoterms 2010). BII shall package and arrange for shipment of Product to the delivery address specified by XENCOR, all in accordance with the instructions of XENCOR provided that BII shall not be responsible for any damages with respect to Product or third party claims arising out of such arrangements for shipment of Product after delivery of such Product to the shipper in accordance with such instructions in accordance with XENCOR's instructions. Each shipment of cGMP Product will include a Certificate of Analysis, a Confirmation of Compliance and such other documentation as reasonably required to meet all applicable statutory and regulatory requirements. Delivery of the Product shall be subject to quality and other provisions set forth in the QAA.
perturbation.justification: The omission of Appendix 6 and Section 2.5 from the definition of 'Specifications' creates a contradiction. The contradicted text refers to 'quality and other provisions set forth in the QAA' for Product delivery. Because the changed text removes the original mechanism for establishing those specifications (Appendix 6 and Section 2.5), there is now no clear contractual basis for determining whether the 'quality' provisions in the QAA are met, leading to a strong contradiction. The contradicted text relies on specifications that the changed text fails to properly define or establish.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1.2 Payment of the Project Fees The Project Fees referred to in Section 3.1.1 above, together with interest at a […***…] percent ([...***...]%) annual interest rate on any unpaid Project Fees accruing from the earlier of (i) the date of completion of the clinical summary report for the Phase 1 clinical trials of the Product as planned according to Appendix 7 unless delayed as described in Section 2.8.3 or (ii) the date that is five (5) calendar years after the Effective Date (each of the alternatives above, the "Due Date") until paid in full (the Project Fees together with any such interest, referred to as the "Total Amount"), shall become duly payable in accordance with the following schedule:
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1.2 Payment of the Project Fees The Project Fees referred to in Section 3.1.1 above, together with interest on any unpaid Project Fees accruing from the earlier of (i) the date of completion of the clinical summary report for the Phase 1 clinical trials of the Product unless delayed as described in Section 2.8.3 or (ii) the date that is five (5) calendar years after the Effective Date (each of the alternatives above, the "Due Date") until paid in full (the Project Fees together with any such interest, referred to as the "Total Amount"), shall become duly payable in accordance with the following schedule:
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the interest rate percentage, this creates an in-text contradiction because the actual interest amount due cannot be calculated according to the terms outlined in Section 3.1.2, leading to uncertainty and unenforceability. This affects the definition of 'Total Amount' in Section 3.1.2 and its subsequent use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Project Fees referred to in Section 3.1.1 above, together with interest on any unpaid Project Fees accruing from the earlier of (i) the date of completion of the clinical summary report for the Phase 1 clinical trials of the Product unless delayed as described in Section 2.8.3 or (ii) the date that is five (5) calendar years after the Effective Date (each of the alternatives above, the "Due Date") until paid in full (the Project Fees together with any such interest, referred to as the "Total Amount"), shall become duly payable in accordance with the following schedule:
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific interest rate percentage, which is essential for calculating the 'Total Amount.' This omission directly contradicts the intention of the original clause and the contradicted text, as it makes determining the payable amount based on interest impossible. Without a specified interest rate, the contract becomes ambiguous and unenforceable regarding the calculation of interest on unpaid fees, leading to a significant in-text contradiction. The definition of the 'Total Amount' is now incomplete and unusable, which falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
